In the beginning, the world was entirely still. The oceans lay flat as glass, and the trees stood frozen, their leaves never trembling. The sky was an empty expanse until Aethel, the great celestial bird, descended from the stars. She possessed feathers woven from pure starlight and eyes like twin moons. Seeking a place to build her nest, Aethel soared across the silent earth. Every beat of her massive wings stirred the stagnant air, creating the very first gentle breezes. 

Eventually, she found the highest peak, Mount Zephyr, and settled there. Yet, the stillness of the world below made her incredibly lonely. To break the silence, Aethel began to sing a sorrowful, echoing melody. As she sang, her grief caused her to stretch her wings and beat them with immense power. Great currents of air rushed down the mountainside, sweeping through the valleys and over the seas. The trees finally danced, their leaves rustling in applause, and the oceans churned with newly born waves. 

Seeing the world come alive, Aethel rejoiced. She realized her true purpose was not to rest, but to keep the earth breathing. To this day, the wind is simply Aethel, flying endlessly to ensure the world never falls still again.
myths.Agent_2:
Long ago, before the mountains had voices, they stood in absolute, heavy silence. The world was ruled by Aethel, the God of Sound, who kept all noises trapped within a grand silver chalice. Birds could not sing, rivers flowed without a murmur, and the wind blew in eerie quiet.

One day, a curious mountain spirit named Echo yearned to hear the rustle of the pine trees that adorned her slopes. While Aethel slept, Echo crept into his celestial hall and tipped the silver chalice. Instantly, a cacophony of sound spilled out, cascading down the heavens and washing over the earth. The rivers began to babble, the wolves howled, and the leaves whispered.

Awakening to the uproar, Aethel was furious. He punished Echo by stripping away her physical form, binding her to the rocky peaks forever. He cursed her to never speak her own thoughts, but only to repeat the sounds of others.

Yet, Echo found peace in her fate. Though she had no voice of her own, she became the eternal companion of the world’s new songs. To this day, if you call out to the mountains, she answers back, sharing the gift of sound.
